title: Tax Incentive For Investment In Bionexus Status Company

DIRECTOR GENERAL'S PUBLIC RULING
Section 138A of the Income Tax Act 1967 (ITA) provides that the Director General is
empowered to make a Public Ruling in relation to the application of any provisions of
the ITA.
A Public Ruling is published as a guide for the public and officers of the Inland
Revenue Board of Malaysia. It sets out the interpretation of the Director General in
respect of the particular tax law and the policy as well as the procedure applicable to
it.
The Director General may withdraw this Public Ruling either wholly or in part, by
notice of withdrawal or by publication of a new Public Ruling.
Director General of Inland Revenue,
Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia.

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
1. Objective
The objective of this Public Ruling (PR) is to explain the tax incentives offered to
an investor who has invested in a BioNexus status company (BSC) in Malaysia.
2. Relevant Provisions of the Law
2.1. This PR takes into account laws which are in force as at the date this PR
is published.
2.2. The provisions of the Income Tax Act 1967 (ITA) related to this PR are
sections 2, 7, and 8.
2.3. The relevant subsidiary legislations referred to in this PR are as follows:
(a) Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status
Company) Rules 2007 [P.U.(A) 373/2007], which are revoked and
replaced by P.U.(A) 306/2016;
(b) Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status
Company) Rules 2016 [P.U.(A) 306/2016] with effect from year of
assessment (YA) 2016;
(c) Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in BioNexus Status
Company) (Amendment) Rules 2022 [P.U.(A) 212/2022], which
came into force on 1 January 2021;
(d) Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in BioNexus Status
Company) (Amendment) Rules 2024 [P.U.(A) 23/2024], which came
into force on 1 January 2023.
3. Interpretation
The words used in this PR have the following meaning:
3.1. “Investment” means—
(a) investment in the form of cash for which there is no obligation for the
investment to be repaid; or
(b) investment in the form of holding of paid-up capital.
3.2. “Investment in the form of holding of paid-up capital” means investment in
the form of holding of paid-up capital which is paid in cash in respect of
ordinary shares.
Page 1 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
3.3. “Resident” means resident in Malaysia for the basis year for a year of
assessment by virtue of section 7 or 8 of the ITA.
3.4. “Chargeable income” in relation to a person and a year of assessment,
means chargeable income ascertained in accordance with the ITA.
3.5. “Statutory income” in relation to a person, a source, and a year of
assessment, means statutory income ascertained in accordance with the
ITA.
3.6. “Adjusted income” in relation to a source and a basis period, means
adjusted income ascertained in accordance with the ITA.
3.7. “Initiation of commercialisation stage” means the stage of research,
assessment and development of an initial concept or prototype before the
technology or product is commercialised, but excludes the increase of
product capacity, product development or product marketing.
3.8. “New business” means the first biotechnology business undertaken by a
BioNexus status company.
3.9. “Related company” has the same meaning assigned to it in section 2 of
the Promotion of Investments Act 1986.
3.10. “BioNexus status company” means a company incorporated under the
Companies Act 2016 [Act 777] including those incorporated under the
Companies Act 1965 [Act 125] which is engaged in a business of life
sciences such as biology, medicine, anthropology, ecology, or any other
branches of science, which deal with living organisms and their
organisation, life processes and relationships to each other and their
environment.
4. Introduction
4.1. The Government has granted a tax incentive for investments at the
initiation of commercialisation stage made by an investor in an approved
BSC to promote biotechnology and bio-based industry. Both the investor
and the BSC would have to comply with the rules relating to the tax
incentive.
4.2. In order to qualify for the tax incentive, all prospective investors are
required to submit an application to the Malaysian Bioeconomy
Development Corporation Sdn. Bhd. (Bioeconomy Corporation) to ensure
that the eligibility criteria are met and certified as approved investors.
Please refer to Bioeconomy Corporation’s website at
www.bioeconomycorporation.my for any further information.
Page 2 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
5. Criteria for Investor
A company or an individual may invest in a BSC which has been approved by
the Minister of Finance. To qualify for a deduction on the amount equivalent to
the actual value of investment made in a BSC, the investor would have to fulfil
the following criteria:
(a) Where the investor is a company—
(i) the company has to be incorporated under the Companies Act 2016
including those incorporated under the Companies Act 1965; and
(ii) its related company has not been allowed the deduction under the
Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status
Company) Rules 2016 [P.U.(A) 306/2016] or Income Tax (Deduction
for Investment in a BioNexus Status Company) Rules 2007 [P.U.(A)
373/2007].
(b) Where the investor is an individual—
(i) the individual has to be a Malaysian citizen, resident and has business
income source;
(ii) only three (3) applications shall be allowed for the purpose of approval
of deduction under the Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in a
BioNexus Status Company) Rules 2016 [P.U.(A) 306/2016];
(iii) each approval shall be granted in respect of an investment made in
three (3) different activities of a new business which are agriculture,
healthcare or industrial; and
(iv) the investments shall be made in a BSC which any of its paid-up
capital is not owned by—
(A) the spouse of the individual;
(B) the brother or sister, of the individual or of the spouse of the
individual;
(C) the parent, of the individual or of the spouse of the individual
(D) the child, including a stepchild or child adopted in accordance
with any law, of the individual;
(E) the grandparent or grandchild, of the individual or of the spouse
of the individual;
Page 3 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
(F) the spouse of any of the individual referred to in
subsubsubparagraph 5(b)(iv)(B); or
(G) the uncle, aunt, or cousin, of the individual or of the spouse of
the individual.
Example 1
Rara, an approved investor is a mother to one of the shareholders in
Halfesto Green Sdn. Bhd., an approved BSC. Rara makes an
investment in Halfesto Green Sdn. Bhd.
For the purpose of the tax incentive on investment in BSC, the
investment made by Rara in Halfesto Green Sdn. Bhd. is not eligible
for the incentive as it is an investment in an investee company of
which one of the shareholders is Rara’s child.
(v) the individual who has obtained an approval for the incentive under
the Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status
Company) Rules 2007 [P.U.(A) 373/2007] may apply for an incentive
under the Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status
Company) Rules 2016 [P.U.(A) 306/2016] provided that the
investment to be made must be in a different activity [as stated in
subparagraph 5(b)(iii) in this PR] from the previous approval.
Example 2
Ramona, an engineer at AEM Moonrise Sdn. Bhd., owns a self-
serviced laundry and derives business income since the year 2012.
She has been granted an approval from the Minister of Finance on
1.2.2014 to invest RM20,000 in Verdezyne Sdn. Bhd., an approved
BSC with pharmaceutical activity, under the Income Tax (Deduction
for Investment in a BioNexus Status Company) Rules 2007 [P.U.(A)
373/2007].
In 2023, she plans to invest in a different activity from the previous
approval. She may apply for an incentive under the Income Tax
(Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status Company) Rules
2016 [P.U.(A) 306/2016] which has been amended by the Income Tax
(Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status Company) Rules
(Amendment) 2024 [P.U.(A) 23/2024] if the new activity falls under
the approved activity other than healthcare.
(c) Applications to make an investment in a BSC must be submitted to the
Minister of Finance through Bioeconomy Corporation for approval on or
after 1.1.2023 but not later than 31.12.2024.
Page 4 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
6. Tax Incentive
6.1. The tax incentive granted to an investor is a deduction from its business
source in ascertaining the adjusted business income. The deduction
allowed in the basis period for a year of assessment is an amount equal
to the actual value of the investment made by the investor in the basis
period in a company approved by the Minister of Finance as a BSC.
6.2. The investment made by the investor has to be made—
(a) for a period not earlier than 1.1.2023 and not later than 31.12.2024;
(b) for the value as approved by the Minister of Finance; and
(c) for the sole purpose of financing activities at the initiation of
commercialisation stage of a new business approved by the Minister
of Finance.
6.3. The investment made by the investors prior to the commencement of a
new business are deemed to be made on the date the new business
commences as determined by Bioeconomy Corporation.
Example 3
CTC Lipido Sdn. Bhd. (CTCL) is a wholly owned subsidiary of CTC
Corporation Bhd. (CTC) and conducts commercial production activities for
structured lipids involving enzymes and purification technology. The
company's financial year ends on 31 December annually. CTCL has been
approved as a BSC on 27.3.2022. CTCL will set up a sophisticated
enzymatic plant supported by research and development (R&D) facilities
in Malaysia for large-scale commercial production of enzymatic structured
lipids. The commencement date for CTCL to carry out the qualifying
business activities is determined on 18.8.2023. CTCL will issue its first
sales invoice on 2.1.2025.
CTC was granted an approval from the Minister of Finance to invest
RM200 million in CTCL under this incentive. The period allowed to make
this investment is for the years 2023 and 2024.
CTC invested 46 million shares @ RM1 per share to CTCL as an initial
investment to finance CTCL's qualified activities in the year 2023 and
additional shares of 154 million shares @ RM1 per share in the year 2024.
CTCL used the paid-up share capital obtained for—
Page 5 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
(i) research, development, assessment, and development of enzyme
products using enzyme modification and purification technology;
and
(ii) capital expenditure and working capital to commence
commercialisation of such products.
CTC has the following sources of income for each year ending 31
December:
YA 2023 YA 2024
Details
RM RM
Adjusted income 300,050,000 560,000,000
Capital allowance 15,000,000 16,500,000
Computation for chargeable income:
YA 2023 YA 2024
Details
RM RM
Adjusted income 300,050,000 560,000,000
Deduction for investment in BSC 46,000,000 154,000,000
Adjusted income 254,050,000 406,000,000
Capital allowance 15,000,000 16,500,000
Statutory income 239,050,000 389,500,000
Tax payable @ 24% 57,372,000 93,480,000
Since CTCL will issue its first sales invoice on 2.1.2025, the deduction
claimed by CTC ceased on that date.
6.4. Investments in the form of holding of paid-up capital should not be
disposed of within five (5) years from the date of the last investment. If
there is any disposal within the five (5) year period, the consideration for
the disposal of such shares has to be added in ascertaining the adjusted
business income of the investor for the year of assessment in the basis
period the consideration is received. The amount added back shall not
exceed the total deduction allowed in relation to the investment in the form
of holding of paid-up capital.
Page 6 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
Example 4
Aqbal has been certified as an approved investor by the Bioeconomy
Corporation. He has invested 500,000 unit of shares @ RM1 per share
from his private funds in a new company, Orien Biotech Sdn. Bhd., on
1.1.2023. Orien Biotech Sdn. Bhd. has been approved as a BSC by the
Bioeconomy Corporation on 2.1.2021, and the investment application was
forwarded to the Minister of Finance for approval. Orien Biotech Sdn. Bhd.
closes its accounts on 31 December each year.
In 2025, due to economic downturn, Aqbal disposed of his shares in Orien
Biotech Sdn. Bhd. at a market value of RM1.50 per share.
Adjusted income from Aqbal’s business:
YA RM
2023 150,000
2024 300,000
2025 20,000
Computation for Aqbal’s adjusted income:
YA 2023 YA 2024 YA 2025
Details
RM RM RM
Adjusted income 150,000 300,000 20,000
Less: Adjusted loss c/f (350,000) (50,000)
Less: Approved
(500,000)
investment in BSC
Add: Sale of investment in
500,000*
BSC
Adjusted income/(loss) (350,000) (50,000) 470,000
Note:
* Proceeds from sale of investment in BSC
500,000 units of shares  RM1.50 market value per share = RM750,000.
Amount to be added back is restricted to RM500,000 (total deduction
allowed for the investment)
Page 7 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
7. Cessation of Tax Incentive
The tax incentive allowed to an investor under the Income Tax Rules as specified
in subparagraph 2.3(b) of this PR ceases in the basis period for a year of
assessment when—
(a) the BSC commences the commercialisation of the activities in respect of
which the investment is made which is based on the date of the first sales
invoice; or
(b) the amount of approved investment has been fully claimed;
whichever is earlier.
8. Non-Application
The Income Tax Rules as specified in subparagraph 2.3(b) of this PR do not
apply to an investor in the basis period for a year of assessment if the investor—
(a) has made an investment in a BSC which has been granted an exemption
under the Income Tax (Exemption) (No. 18) Order 2007 [P.U.(A) 372/2007];
(b) has made a claim for an allowance under Schedule 7A or Schedule 7B of
the ITA;
(c) has been granted any incentive under the Promotion of Investments Act
1986;
(d) has been granted an exemption under section 127 of the ITA; or
(e) has made a claim for deductions under any Rules made under section 154
of the ITA except—
(i) allowance under Schedule 3 to the ITA;
(ii) the Income Tax (Deduction for Audit Expenditure) Rules 2006
[P.U.(A) 129/2006];
(iii) the Income Tax (Deductions for Cost relating to Training for
Employees for the Implementation of Goods and Services Tax) Rules
2014 [P.U.(A) 334/2014]; or
(iv) Income Tax (Deduction for Expenses in relation to Secretarial Fees
and Tax Filing Fee) Rules 2020 [P.U.(A) 162/2020] and Income Tax
(Deduction for Expenses in relation to Secretarial Fee and Tax Filing
Fee) (Amendment) Rules 2021 [P.U.(A) 471/2021].
Page 8 of 9

TAX INCENTIVE FOR INVESTMENT IN
BIONEXUS STATUS COMPANY
Public Ruling No. 5/2024
INLAND REVENUE BOARD OF MALAYSIA Date of Publication: 27 December 2024
9. Revocation
The Income Tax (Deduction for Investment in a BioNexus Status Company)
Rules 2007 [P.U.(A) 373/2007] which has been revoked, shall continue to apply
to deduction for investment which has been approved under that Rules as if that
Rules have not been revoked.
10. Updates and Amendments
This PR replaces PR No.2/2023 dated 4 October 2023. The content of this PR
has been amended and updated as follows:
Paragraph Explanation
2 Subparagraph 2.3 is amended by inserting new
subsubparagraph 2.3(d).
5 Subparagraph 5(b)(v) is amended.
Example 2 is updated.
Subparagraph 5(c) is amended.
6 Subsubparagraph 6.2(a) is amended.
Examples 3 and 4 are updated.
8 Subsubparagraph 8(e)(iv) is amended.
11. Disclaimer
The examples in this PR are for illustration purposes only and are not exhaustive.
This PR should be read together with PR No. 1/2020 (Second Edition) dated 22
May 2020 on Tax Incentives for BioNexus Status Companies which is available
at www.hasil.gov.my, the official portal of IRBM.
Director General of Inland Revenue,
Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia.
Page 9 of 9
